title: viola epipsila subsp. repens
intro: Viola epipsila subsp. repens, also known as Wood Sorrel or Creeping Violet, is a subspecies of perennial herb that belongs to the violet family. It is used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a natural diuretic and to treat urinary tract infections due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Viola epipsila subsp. repens include reducing inflammation, acting as an antiseptic, and treating digestive issues such as diarrhea and indigestion. The plant can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and its leaves can be used in salads or as a garnish, although caution should be exercised as it contains oxalic acid and may be toxic in large quantities.
